name: Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà
region_intro: Mountain biking around Organyà, nestled in the Catalan pre-Pyrenees, offers diverse terrain and scenic landscapes. The region features rugged mountain ranges, lush valleys, and varied geological formations, providing a challenging environment for mountain bikers. Trails often include technical paths, significant climbs, and varied descents with blocks and slab sections. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and varied topography, making it suitable for different riding styles. Best mountain bike trails around Organyà The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà, a 42.6 miles (68.5 km) trail that takes 7 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente de las Masias loop from Organyà, a difficult 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route offers varied terrain with technical sections and scenic vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the Collada de la Creu de Ferri – Coll de Paller loop from Organyà, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading through rugged mountain landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Organyà is defined by technical paths, varied descents, and panoramic vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Organyà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 8 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Organyà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Organyà?
A: Organyà, nestled in the Catalan pre-Pyrenees, offers diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter rugged mountain ranges, lush valleys, and varied geological formations. Trails often feature technical paths, significant climbs, and varied descents with blocks and slab sections, providing a challenging environment for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Organyà?
A: While many routes in Organyà are rated difficult, there are some moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa del Carrabina – Dolmen del Molí de Fava loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that covers about 27.3 km. It offers a less strenuous option for those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Organyà?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can find idyllic spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4689400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls of Organyà&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a refreshing stop. Additionally, mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/762069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bóixols&lt;/a&gt; provide incredible landscapes and panoramic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Organyà?
A: The mountain bike trails around Organyà are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the technical paths, varied descents, and panoramic vistas that define the riding experience in this region. The diverse terrain and challenging routes are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Organyà?
A: Yes, Organyà offers several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950358108/sierra-de-boumort-ruta-circular-desde-organya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 68.5 km trail with significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de la Creu de Ferri – Coll de Paller loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.6 km trail through rugged mountain landscapes.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Organyà?
A: Mountain biking around Organyà is generally characterized by difficult and technical paths. Out of the 12 available tours, 9 are rated as difficult, indicating that many routes present significant physical and technical challenges. There are also 3 moderate routes for those seeking a less intense experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Organyà?
A: The Catalan pre-Pyrenees region, where Organyà is located, offers varied terrain. While specific weather conditions can vary, generally, spring and autumn provide the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, making refreshing spots like the Waterfalls of Organyà particularly appealing.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Organyà?
A: Yes, there are several long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950358108/sierra-de-boumort-ruta-circular-desde-organya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 68.5 km trail, typically taking over 7 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31439426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Boumort loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 61 km.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Organyà?
A: The trails around Organyà are known for significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950358108/sierra-de-boumort-ruta-circular-desde-organya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Boumort — Ruta circular desde Organyà&lt;/a&gt; involves over 2,100 meters of ascent. Even shorter difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de la Creu de Ferri – Coll de Paller loop from Organyà&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1,300 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in Organyà offer panoramic vistas. The region's geography provides incredible landscapes and views, especially from higher elevations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/762069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bóixols&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass. Trails often include sections with sweeping views of the rugged mountain ranges and lush valleys.

Q: What amenities are available for cyclists in Organyà?
A: Organyà supports its cycling culture with local amenities. You can find bars, supermarkets, and bakeries in the town, making it convenient for riders to resupply or relax after a ride. The trails themselves are often well-maintained, though some sections may require navigational skills.
tips: Small stone shelter. It is located in the Sierra de Boumort, near the homonymous peak. It's basically a survival shelter.

In the middle of a beautiful natural setting, at the foot of the Sierra de Sant Joan, we find this beautiful church, in Romanesque style and from the 12th century.
Located in the municipality of Coll de Nargó, in the region of La Noguera.
It is located on top of a hill. The environment that surrounds the church and also the town, is beautiful. The church is documented from the second half of the 10th century.
